nativeRange.habitat: Wet soils, shallow waters, marshes, rice paddies, seasonally flooded wetlands, and slow-flowing streams. Most aquarium species come from soft, acidic, mineral-poor waters of South and South-East Asia.

decorPreferences: open space around rosette for growth

summary: A genus of demanding rosette plants from Eriocaulaceae (Poales) prized in aquascaping for their star-shaped form. Most aquarium species are slow-growing, intolerant of hard or alkaline water, and require soft acidic conditions, strong light, CO2 injection, and nutrient-rich substrate to thrive.
careNotes: Eriocaulon is a large genus of ~500 species (Wikipedia, 2026) with nearly cosmopolitan distribution, but only a handful are kept in the aquarium hobby: E. cinereum, E. quinquangulare, E. setaceum, E. ratnagiricum, E. polaris, and E. parkeri are the most common in trade. All share demanding husbandry: soft acidic water (pH ~5.0-7.0, kH 0-7, gH 0-8), warm temperature (20-28 °C), high lighting (Flowgrow specifies '0.75+ watts per liter' for E. setaceum, generally PAR 60+), and pressurized CO2 (25-40 mg/L per Flowgrow data on E. cinereum and E. setaceum). The plants rely heavily on root absorption from a nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil such as ADA Amazonia, Tropica Soil, or UNS Controsoil) supplemented with iron-rich root tabs; column dosing of macros (NO3 10-50 mg/L, PO4 0.1-3 mg/L, K) and micros (especially iron and trace elements) is also essential. Species-level adult size varies widely: E. cinereum stays compact (~8 cm) and works as a foreground/midground accent or nano-tank plant, while E. setaceum reaches ~30 cm and is used as a background hairgrass-like plant; the genus average is captured here as ~5-30 cm. Eriocaulon is notoriously sensitive to parameter swings — leaf melt and rosette collapse can follow drastic changes in pH, temperature, or substrate disturbance. Tissue-cultured starts transition more reliably than emersed-grown nursery stock. Hard, alkaline tap water (gH/kH > 8) is incompatible with most species; RO/DI water remineralized for soft conditions is strongly recommended. Cross-source disagreement on care level is minimal — Flowgrow rates both E. cinereum and E. setaceum as 'Difficult', and the genus consensus is firmly advanced/expert-tier. This is a genus-level entry covering shared husbandry; species-specific entries should be added later for E. cinereum (true foreground), E. setaceum (background hairgrass-form), E. quinquangulare, and E. ratnagiricum (Indian endemic, popular Dutch-style accent), as their adult sizes and placements diverge meaningfully.
breedingNotes: Most Eriocaulon species propagate vegetatively via splitting the mother rosette into daughter plants once side shoots develop at the base, or by cutting off naturally produced daughter plants. Stem-form species (E. setaceum) propagate by cuttings — Flowgrow recommends a maximum cutting length of 5 cm and warns that the plant 'does not respond well to trimming', so cut only when necessary. Emersed-grown plants flower readily and produce small wind-pollinated flowers on tall stalks; seed propagation is possible but uncommon in the aquarium hobby and slow.

primary.name: Flowgrow (Eriocaulon cinereum)
primary.notes: Primary data source for water parameters (temp 18-30 °C, pH 4.5-7, kH 0-7), lighting (high), CO2 (25-40 mg/L), growth rate (slow), placement (foreground/midground/nano), max height (~8 cm), care level (difficult), native range (South-East Asia rice paddies; widespread Africa, Asia, Australia), and propagation (seeds, splitting, daughter plants). Used as anchor because Buce Plant's E. cinereum product page returned HTTP 404.

additional.name: Wikipedia (Eriocaulon)
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (family Eriocaulaceae, order Poales), genus species count (~500 species, 496 accepted as of May 2026), global distribution (Americas, British Isles, sub-Saharan Africa, central/east/south/south-east Asia), morphology (rosette herbs resembling Cyperaceae/Juncaceae), habitat (wet soils, shallow water, wetlands, flatwoods), and ecology ('weak competitors compared to many other wetland plant species'). No aquarium-specific data.

additional.name: Flowgrow (Eriocaulon setaceum)
additional.notes: Cross-checked genus husbandry on a second species: temp 20-30 °C, pH 4.5-7, kH 0-7, lighting high (0.75+ watts/L), CO2 25-40 mg/L (required), growth medium, placement background/midground, care difficult, native South-East Asia and Australia. Confirmed iron and trace-element fertilization, nitrate 10-50 mg/L, phosphate 0.1-3 mg/L. Propagation by cuttings up to 5 cm; plant 'does not respond well to trimming'.
